notes.title: Informed ML Survey
notes.summary: This note tracks the core taxonomy of informed machine learning through knowledge source, representation, and integration location, with emphasis on the distinctions that remain useful for later comparison.

notes.title: Logic-Net
notes.summary: A rule-regularized teacher distribution is constructed first and then distilled back into student parameters, making the note useful for understanding posterior shaping, distillation, and optimization-level constraint injection.

notes.title: Semantic Loss
notes.summary: This note centers on the 2018 Semantic Loss formulation where a propositional constraint is turned into a trainable loss through the probability mass of satisfying Boolean assignments.

notes.title: DL2
notes.summary: This note focuses on how DL2 turns logical constraints into a unified declarative interface that can shape both training and querying rather than only a single loss term.

notes.title: Granular Computing for ML
notes.summary: This note tracks the argument that machine learning outputs should often be represented as granules with coverage, specificity, and uncertainty rather than a single over-precise point estimate.

notes.title: From Fuzzy Rule-Based Models to Granular Models
notes.summary: This note explains how a fuzzy rule-based model can move from crisp consequents to interval, fuzzy, or probabilistic granules, changing both output semantics and uncertainty expression.

notes.title: Knowledge Landmarks
notes.summary: This note follows how input-output landmark pairs and conditional fuzzy clustering become a knowledge-aware regularizer, with attention to local guidance, counterfactual meaning, and failure modes.
